title: tamaricaceae
intro: The tamaricaceae rank, also known as the tamarix family, is a taxonomic rank that includes a group of flowering plant families characterized by their distinctive, shrubby or small tree growth habit, and small, scale-like leaves. This family is primarily composed of salt-tolerant plants, commonly found in arid and coastal regions worldwide. The tamaricaceae family encompasses approximately 90-100 species, including the iconic tamarisk tree. These plants are often valued for their ability to stabilize soil, prevent erosion, and provide habitat for various wildlife species.
